definition: Relating to or denoting an increase or addition, especially one that is small or gradual.
example: We don't do incremental. We do what's next. We ship. We deliver.

definition: The state of being superior; dominance or excellence.
example: Superiority is the aim: building the most advanced technology not to follow, but to lead.

definition: Able to move quickly and easily; nimble.
example: If you're light and agile, you close faster, learn faster, and survive long enough to chase bigger budgets later.

definition: A project or undertaking, especially a bold or complex one; a business or company.
example: Enterprise lives in the database—the heart of everything—and we will make it the most powerful heart in the industry.
Dominance goes to those who learn faster than anyone else.

definition: The ability to move quickly and easily; nimbleness or adaptability.
example: Agility belongs to people who change industries by learning faster than the environment around them.

definition: The carrying out or putting into effect of a plan, order, or course of action.
example: Execution rejects the incremental: we do what’s next; we ship; we deliver.

definition: The action of leading a group of people or an organization.
example: Leadership builds the most advanced technology in the world—not to follow, but to lead.

definition: A structured set of data held in a computer, accessible in various ways.
example: He treated the database as industrial infrastructure—the silent heart of modern enterprise.

definition: To strive to gain or win something by defeating others.
example: To compete at the top is to learn faster than the market can copy you.

definition: On-demand computing resources delivered over the internet.
example: The cloud is not a place—it is an operating model that punishes hesitation.

definition: The use of technology to perform tasks with minimal human intervention.
example: Automation without judgment creates faster mistakes; pair it with accountability.

definition: Without pity or compassion; relentlessly severe (in pursuit of goals).
example: A ruthless focus on the mission can still leave room for loyalty to people who ship.

definition: The complex structure of a system; the art of designing structures.
example: Software architecture is destiny: choose poorly and every feature becomes a tax.

definition: The action of carrying out a task; measurable efficiency of a system.
example: Performance is a feature: users feel latency as disrespect.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.
